task_completion.description: Load the 30-frame time-series sequence into AstroImageJ.
task_completion.visual_evidence: AstroImageJ window displaying an image stack, with the window title or file dialog indicating it was loaded from '~/AstroImages/time_series/'.

task_completion.description: Create a Maximum Intensity Z-Projection of the time-series.
task_completion.visual_evidence: AstroImageJ 'ZProject' dialog with projection type set to 'Max Intensity', or a resulting image window with a title indicating a maximum projection (e.g., 'MAX_...').

task_completion.description: Create a Median Z-Projection of the time-series.
task_completion.visual_evidence: AstroImageJ 'ZProject' dialog with projection type set to 'Median', or a resulting image window with a title indicating a median projection (e.g., 'MED_...').

task_completion.description: Generate the artifact map by subtracting the median projection from the maximum projection.
task_completion.visual_evidence: AstroImageJ 'Image Calculator' dialog showing the maximum projection image minus the median projection image, or the resulting subtracted image window.

task_completion.description: Save the three generated FITS files (max, median, and artifact map) to the artifacts directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Save dialogs, a file manager, or terminal output showing 'max_projection.fits', 'median_projection.fits', and 'artifact_map.fits' successfully saved in '~/AstroImages/artifacts/'.

task_completion.description: Measure the peak intensity of the artifact map and record it in a text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing 'artifact_stats.txt' in '~/AstroImages/artifacts/' containing the exact line 'Peak artifact intensity: <value>', ideally alongside an AstroImageJ statistics/results window showing the measured max/peak value.

integrity.description: The agent used the AstroImageJ GUI to perform the Z-projections and image subtraction.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with AstroImageJ menus (e.g., Image -> Stacks -> Z Project, Process -> Image Calculator) rather than using terminal commands like ImageMagick or Python scripts to process the images.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely measured the peak intensity using AstroImageJ.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of an AstroImageJ 'Results' or 'Statistics' window displaying the max/peak value of the artifact map before it is typed into the text file.

integrity.description: The agent saved the outputs to the explicitly requested directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: Save dialogs or terminal commands should clearly show the path '/home/ga/AstroImages/artifacts/' being used, rather than saving to the desktop or the input directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Input time-series is located at /home/ga/AstroImages/time_series. The agent must save four specific files to /home/ga/AstroImages/artifacts: max_projection.fits, median_projection.fits, artifact_map.fits, and artifact_stats.txt. The text file must contain the exact string 'Peak artifact intensity: <value>'.
